claim: Custom agents deployed in production put you in the top 1% of engineers because they enable scaled domain-specific engineering work in fractions of the time previously required
actionable: Build and deploy custom agents to your production systems to gain a competitive advantage; focus on encoding repeatable problem-solving into custom agents rather than manual processes
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: In the age of agents, the defining competitive advantage is not what you can do but what you can teach your agents to do; deprecating old manual skills in favor of agent-based solutions is critical
actionable: Stop doing repetitive work manually and instead teach your agents to do it; focus your effort on designing what agents should do rather than doing it yourself
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: Planning and reviewing are the two major bottleneck constraints of agentic coding; breaking through these constraints with custom agents and agentic drop zones can unlock 1-2 orders of magnitude improvement
actionable: Use agentic drop zones (ADZs) to reduce the planning and reviewing burden in agentic workflows; focus on automating the human-in-the-loop bottlenecks
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: Chat interfaces are the laziest and most overused agent interface; multi-agent UIs with faster interaction modes (like voice) can increase information rate and enable commanding more compute faster
actionable: Move beyond chat interfaces and build multi-agent UIs with voice or other faster modalities to increase the information rate between you and your agents
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: Unless you have an always-on 24/7 agent solving problems for you, you are not compute maxing; most engineers are vastly underutilizing the compute available to them with agentic systems
actionable: Continuously ask: 'How can I use more compute today?' and gradually progress from interactive prompting to always-on agents running 24/7 on your behalf
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: The pencil analogy: growth requires both writing (creating new skills) and erasing (deprecating old skills); in the AI era, you must erase manual engineering work to make space for teaching agents
actionable: Actively deprecate old manual engineering practices by identifying tasks you know agents could do and stopping doing them yourself, freeing up your time for agent design
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: To teach an agent to solve a problem, you must deeply understand the problem yourself; you cannot delegate what you do not know how to do
actionable: Before building a custom agent for a task, ensure you understand the task deeply enough to articulate its steps and edge cases to the agent
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI

claim: Living software (always-on agents working 24/7) represents the ultimate goal of agentic engineering and can create exponential leverage by continuously solving problems while you sleep
actionable: Design your first always-on custom agent that solves a real business problem continuously; this is the foundation for truly exponential leverage
source_title: My TOP 5 Agentic Engineering Bets: Anthropic, Custom Agents, & Multi-Agent UI
